disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Clear watery fluid is leaking from a newly broken-open area on the midline lump at the back of the infant's head
trigger_question_examples: What have you noticed about the lump today?
Is anything coming out of the broken skin?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: There's clear, thin, watery fluid dripping out of a raw spot at the top of the lump. It started about four hours ago and hasn't stopped.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: There is a new area of skin breakdown / a raw patch over the apex of the sac that was not there before
trigger_question_examples: What does the skin over the lump look like?
Is the skin over it broken?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The skin right at the top of the lump looks rubbed raw and broken — it was intact yesterday. We think it rubbed on the mattress.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The infant has become increasingly irritable with a new high-pitched cry over several hours
trigger_question_examples: Has his crying or behaviour changed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's been crying much more than normal, and it's a high-pitched cry we haven't heard before. He seems really unsettled.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The lump is a swelling present at the back of the head since birth, awaiting a neurosurgical review (known baseline congenital finding)
trigger_question_examples: Has he had this lump before?
What have the doctors said about it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He was born with the lump at the back of his head. The doctors said it needs surgery and we're waiting for the appointment — but it has never leaked or broken open until today.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The infant settles when held prone and cries more when supine (positional distress relating to the sac)
trigger_question_examples: Is he more settled in any particular position?
Does lying him down a certain way change the crying?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's most upset lying on his back and calms down a bit when we hold him on his front, off the back of his head.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever felt by parents, no observed seizures, and not floppy/unrousable at present (pertinent negatives at this early stage)
trigger_question_examples: Has he had a fever or any jerking movements?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He doesn't feel hot to us and we haven't seen any jerking or fits. He's very upset but he's awake and reacting to us.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: We haven't taken his temperature — we don't have a working thermometer.
